objective: Improve fail-fast retrieval for workflow/doc-heavy governance scenarios by adding a deterministic hybrid retrieval path that preserves graph-guided owner discovery while elevating workflow and documentation artifacts when the scenario depends on them.
scope_boundary: This slice is limited to governance-owned retrieval/evaluation tooling, scenario corpus updates, and guidance updates for workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast issues.
This slice may add a deterministic hybrid retrieval path that combines graph-guided owner discovery with bounded workflow/doc artifact ranking.
This slice may update measured artifacts and guidance if the hybrid retrieval path materially improves at least one workflow/doc-heavy scenario versus the issue #66 result.
out_of_scope: Broad graph UX/reporting work such as PR comments, sweep dashboards, or adoption visualizations.
Neo4j changes, semantic extraction, or model fine-tuning.
research_summary: Issue #66 proved graph-guided retrieval can beat bounded repo search on code-centric fail-fast debugging, but it still underperformed on workflow/doc-heavy cases such as reusable workflow debugging and sweep persistence analysis. Those misses are caused less by graph ranking and more by corpus shape: the graph is strongest on code ownership, while the decisive evidence for these scenarios lives in workflow YAML, plan docs, and metrics artifacts. The right next move is a deterministic hybrid retrieval path that keeps graph-guided retrieval first for owning-file discovery, then layers a bounded workflow/doc artifact search on top for those scenario types.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Implement and validate a workflow/doc-aware hybrid retrieval path.
tasks: Add a deterministic hybrid retrieval layer for workflow-heavy scenarios that ranks .github/workflows, docs/, metrics/, and related artifacts alongside graph-guided results.
Re-run the existing fail-fast corpus and verify at least one workflow/doc-heavy scenario improves versus the issue #66 result.
Update governance guidance to document when workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast work should use hybrid retrieval instead of graph-only retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: At least one workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast scenario materially improves versus the issue #66 measurement.
Governance documents the preferred retrieval path for workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast issues.
Any remaining unresolved gap is either finished in this slice or recorded as an issue-backed follow-up before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep issue #85 focused on workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast retrieval rather than broad graph UX or graph quality work.
specialist_reviews.summary: The approved direction is to keep graphify as the owner/topology layer and add a bounded deterministic hybrid retrieval path only for workflow/doc-heavy fail-fast scenarios.

specialist_reviews.focus: Reuse the issue #66 evaluator and corpus so any improvement is directly comparable to the previous measurement.
specialist_reviews.summary: The current evaluator and scenario corpus already provide a valid comparison point. The right move is to extend them with a hybrid path rather than replace the evaluation flow.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice is narrow and deterministic enough to proceed without an alternate-model review.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement a deterministic workflow/doc hybrid retrieval path in governance, re-run the fail-fast corpus, and update guidance if the measured result improves.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Patch the evaluator to augment graph-guided results with bounded workflow/doc artifact ranking, then re-run the fail-fast corpus and compare against the issue #66 outputs.

material_deviation_rules: If the hybrid retrieval path still cannot materially improve at least one workflow/doc-heavy scenario, do not silently widen the slice into upstream graphify or broader graph UX work; record the result and create or update a follow-up issue instead.
If new required work is discovered during Review/Adjust, either finish it in the current slice or create an issue-backed follow-up before closing issue #85.
Do not add a second non-graph baseline in this slice unless the hybrid path remains inconclusive after the direct issue #66 comparison.
